title: melampyrum sylvaticum subsp. carpaticum
intro: Melampyrum sylvaticum subsp. carpaticum, also known as the Carpathian monkey flower, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae. This plant is known for its unique ability to obtain nutrients from surrounding plants, making it a hemiparasitic species. Melampyrum sylvaticum subsp. carpaticum has been found to have medicinal properties, including its ability to stimulate digestion, reduce inflammation, and act as an expectorant. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis, coughs, and colds, and is typically consumed in the form of tea or infusions.
